meta.title: Engine Start System
meta.description: Create a simulated engine start system with a switch, button, LED, and buzzer.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding basic input/output control with switches and sensors
meta.code_hint: Check slide switch state, activate LED and buzzer when button is pressed

meta.title: Engine Start System
meta.description: Create a simulated engine start system with a switch, button, LED, and buzzer.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding basic input/output control with switches and sensors
meta.code_hint: Check slide switch state, activate LED and buzzer when button is pressed

walkthrough.instruction: Place the slide switch on the breadboard with the centre pin in row 12, column E and the side pin in row 13, column E.
walkthrough.tip: Slide it toward the GND side to turn ON — that closes the circuit.

walkthrough.instruction: Place the button onto the breadboard across the center gap. Top legs in row 17, columns E and F. Bottom legs in row 19, columns E and F.
walkthrough.tip: The button bridges the two halves of the breadboard.

walkthrough.instruction: Put the red LED on the breadboard with the long leg (anode) in row 25, column E and the short leg (cathode) in row 24, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The long leg is positive — it's called the anode!

walkthrough.instruction: Put the 220Ω resistor on the breadboard with one leg in row 24, column D and the other leg in row 20, column D.
walkthrough.tip: The resistor limits current so the component doesn't burn out.

walkthrough.instruction: Place the buzzer with the long leg (+) in row 26, column F and the short leg (−) in row 29, column F.
walkthrough.tip: The long leg is positive — mark it with a tiny + on the buzzer.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D9 to the breadboard at row 12, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 13, column D to the negative (−) rail at row 13.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D7 to the breadboard at row 17, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 19, column G to the negative (−) rail at row 19.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D5 to the breadboard at row 25, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 20, column E to the negative (−) rail at row 20.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D2 to the breadboard at row 26, column J.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 29, column G to the negative (−) rail at row 29.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the Arduino's GND pin to the negative (−) rail at row 30.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.
